paper.title: Massive runaway star HD 254577: the pre-supernova binary companion to the progenitor of the supernova remnant IC 443

extraction.summary: The paper studies HD 254577 as a massive runaway star inside SNR IC 443 (peculiar 3-D velocity 31.3 km/s). It does not present any object as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential.

candidate_groups_considered.description: HD 254577, the massive runaway star inside SNR IC 443 discussed in this paper, together with the associated neutron star CXOU J61705.3+222127.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents HD 254577 as a runaway star ejected from a pre-supernova binary inside SNR IC 443, with a peculiar 3-D velocity of 31.3 km/s. It does not treat the star (or any associated object) as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential. The velocity is characteristic of runaway stars, not hypervelocity or Galactic-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Abstract and conclusions state the paper presents the massive runaway star HD 254577 with a peculiar 3-D velocity of 31.3 km/s, without any claim of Galactic-unbound status.
Summary section explicitly describes HD 254577 as a runaway star linked to SNR IC 443, with a 3-D peculiar velocity of 31.3 km/s, and does not discuss escaping the Galaxy.
